114	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-016): the substantive functor mirroring
115	Mathlib's `Sheaf.cohomologyPresheafFunctor` for the `ModuleCat k` flavor. Sends
116	a sheaf `F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)` to the presheaf
117	`X ↦ Ext^n((presheafToSheaf J _).obj ((yoneda ⋙ ModuleCat.free k).obj X), F)`,
118	i.e.\ to a presheaf `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` whose value at `op X` is
119	`HModule' k F n X` (definitionally, see `HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf` below).
120	
121	The codomain is `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` (not `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat k`) because Mathlib's
122	`Abelian.extFunctor n : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ C ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` always lands in `AddCommGrpCat`
123	regardless of the source category's `Linear`-enrichment; the per-fiber `Module k`
124	structure on `(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (op X)` is preserved
125	through the iter-014 `HModule'` reducibility chain. Probe-confirmed body
126	(iter-016 plan-agent). Used downstream as the prerequisite for the queued
127	iter-017+ `ModuleCat k`-flavored Mayer-Vietoris LES. -/
128	noncomputable def HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor
129	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
130	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
131	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
132	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
133	    (n : ℕ) :
134	    Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k) ⥤ Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat :=
135	  Functor.flip
136	    ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k) ⋙
137	      presheafToSheaf _ _).op ⋙ Abelian.extFunctor n)
138	
139	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-016): the abbrev wrapper mirroring Mathlib's
140	`Sheaf.cohomologyPresheaf` for the `ModuleCat k` flavor. Evaluates the
141	`HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor` at a sheaf `F`, giving a presheaf
142	`Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` whose value at `op X` is `HModule' k F n X` definitionally
143	(probe-confirmed `rfl` by the iter-016 plan-agent). The `noncomputable abbrev`
144	form is required for the per-fiber definitional identification with `HModule'`;
145	under `def` the wrapper would block the `rfl`-level reduction that downstream
146	Mayer-Vietoris and Stein-factorization arguments rely on. -/
147	noncomputable abbrev HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf
148	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
149	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
150	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
151	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
152	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
153	    Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat :=
154	  (HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor k J n).obj F
155	
156	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-017): the first Mayer-Vietoris LES building
157	block on the `ModuleCat k` side — the sum of the two restriction maps
158	`(cohomologyPresheaf F n).map S.f₂₄.op` and `(cohomologyPresheaf F n).map S.f₃₄.op`,
159	as a single map into the biproduct. Direct mirror of Mathlib's
160	`GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.toBiprod` (file
161	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`, L43–46) for
162	the `ModuleCat k` flavor with `F.cohomologyPresheaf → HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F`.
163	
164	The codomain `(... ).obj (op S.X₂) ⊞ (...).obj (op S.X₃)` is the biproduct in
165	`AddCommGrpCat` (Mathlib `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/Grp/Biproducts.lean`); since
166	`HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n` is `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat`, the biproduct
167	auto-resolves. Probe-confirmed body (iter-017 plan-agent). Used downstream as the
168	first of three Mayer-Vietoris LES building blocks; iter-018 will add the
169	composition-is-zero lemma and the connecting hom `δ`, iter-019 the LES sequence
170	and exactness theorem. -/
171	noncomputable def HModule'_toBiprod
172	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
173	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
174	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
175	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
176	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
177	    (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₄) ⟶
178	      (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂) ⊞
179	        (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₃) :=
180	  biprod.lift ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₂₄.op)
181	              ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₃₄.op)
182	
183	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-017): the second Mayer-Vietoris LES building
184	block on the `ModuleCat k` side — the difference of the two restriction maps
185	`(cohomologyPresheaf F n).map S.f₁₂.op` and `(cohomologyPresheaf F n).map S.f₁₃.op`,
186	as a single map out of the biproduct. Direct mirror of Mathlib's
187	`GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.fromBiprod` (file
188	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`, L67–70) for
189	the `ModuleCat k` flavor.
190	
191	The negation `-(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₃.op` uses the
192	preadditive structure on `AddCommGrpCat`-morphisms; the sign is the standard
193	Mayer-Vietoris convention encoding the alternating-sum structure of the Čech
194	complex. Probe-confirmed body (iter-017 plan-agent). -/
195	noncomputable def HModule'_fromBiprod
196	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
197	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
198	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
199	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
200	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
201	    (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂) ⊞
202	        (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₃) ⟶
203	      (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₁) :=
204	  biprod.desc ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₂.op)
205	              (-(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₃.op)
206	
207	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-018): the third Mayer-Vietoris LES building
208	block on the `ModuleCat k` side — the composition-is-zero lemma asserting
209	`HModule'_toBiprod k S F n ≫ HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n = 0`. Direct mirror
210	of Mathlib's `GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.toBiprod_fromBiprod`
211	(file `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`,
212	L72–75) for the `ModuleCat k` flavor.
213	
214	The proof unwinds via the biproduct universal property
215	(`biprod.lift_desc : biprod.lift a b ≫ biprod.desc c d = a ≫ c + b ≫ d`),
216	the preadditive negation (`Preadditive.comp_neg`), the addition-of-negation
217	arithmetic (`← sub_eq_add_neg`), the zero-iff-equal arithmetic
218	(`sub_eq_zero`), the contravariant functoriality of `cohomologyPresheaf`
219	(`← Functor.map_comp`, `← op_comp`), and the Mayer-Vietoris square
220	factorisation `S.toSquare.fac` (which says `S.f₁₂ ≫ S.f₂₄ = S.f₁₃ ≫ S.f₃₄`).
221	Probe-confirmed proof (iter-018 plan-agent); the `simp only` set transfers
222	verbatim from Mathlib's `AddCommGrpCat`-flavored proof because every step
223	is value-category-agnostic.
224	
225	The `@[reassoc (attr := simp)]` attribute generates the post-composition
226	form `… ≫ HModule'_fromBiprod … ≫ Z = 0 ≫ Z = 0` and registers the lemma
227	as a `simp` lemma; both are required for downstream Mayer-Vietoris LES
228	exactness arguments. -/
229	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
230	lemma HModule'_toBiprod_fromBiprod
231	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
232	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
233	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
234	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
235	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
236	    HModule'_toBiprod k S F n ≫ HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n = 0 := by
237	  simp only [HModule'_toBiprod, HModule'_fromBiprod, biprod.lift_desc,
238	    Preadditive.comp_neg, ← sub_eq_add_neg, sub_eq_zero,
239	    ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, S.toSquare.fac]
240	
241	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-019 helper, Mathlib gap-fill): `ModuleCat.free k`
242	is left adjoint to `forget (ModuleCat k)`. Mathlib's
243	`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Adjunctions.lean` provides the adjunction
244	`ModuleCat.adj k : ModuleCat.free k ⊣ forget (ModuleCat k)` but does not register
245	the corresponding `IsLeftAdjoint` instance (whereas `AddCommGrpCat.free.IsLeftAdjoint`
246	is registered in `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/Grp/Adjunctions.lean` L84). This
247	project-local instance fills the gap so that `Sheaf.composeAndSheafify J (ModuleCat.free k)`
248	acquires the necessary `PreservesColimit` instance, used by
249	`HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf` below. -/
250	instance ModuleCat_free_isLeftAdjoint
251	    (k : Type u) [Field k] : (ModuleCat.free k).IsLeftAdjoint :=
252	  ⟨_, ⟨ModuleCat.adj k⟩⟩
253	
254	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-019): the `ModuleCat k`-flavored
255	pushout-of-free-sheaves analog of Mathlib's
256	`GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.isPushoutAddCommGrpFreeSheaf`
257	(file `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/MayerVietorisSquare.lean`, L154–160).
258	States that the image of the Mayer-Vietoris square `S` under the composite
259	`yoneda ⋙ Functor.whiskeringRight ⋅ (ModuleCat.free k) ⋙ presheafToSheaf J _`
260	is a pushout square in `Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`. The proof transfers the
261	type-level pushout `S.isPushout` through `Sheaf.composeAndSheafify J (ModuleCat.free k)`
262	(which preserves pushouts because `(ModuleCat.free k).IsLeftAdjoint`, registered
263	in `ModuleCat_free_isLeftAdjoint` above) and adjusts via the canonical iso
264	`presheafToSheafCompComposeAndSheafifyIso`.
265	
266	Probe-confirmed body (iter-019 plan-agent); the proof is verbatim from Mathlib L156–160
267	with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`. Used downstream in iter-020+ to
268	prove `Mono` / `Epi` / `Exact` instances on the iter-019 short complex
269	`HModule'_shortComplex`, and ultimately in the iter-022+ `δ` connecting hom. -/
270	lemma HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf
271	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
272	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
273	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
274	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
275	    (S.map (yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k) ⋙
276	      presheafToSheaf J _)).IsPushout :=
277	  (S.isPushout.map (Sheaf.composeAndSheafify J (ModuleCat.free k))).of_iso
278	    ((Square.mapFunctor.mapIso
279	      (presheafToSheafCompComposeAndSheafifyIso J (ModuleCat.free k))).app
280	        (S.map yoneda))
281	
282	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-019): the `ModuleCat k`-flavored
283	short complex of free sheaves underlying the Mayer-Vietoris LES.
284	Direct mirror of Mathlib's `GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.shortComplex`
285	(file `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/MayerVietorisSquare.lean`, L234–249) for the
286	`ModuleCat k` flavor with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`.
287	
288	The objects are the sheafified free `ModuleCat k`-valued presheaves on the four
289	vertices of the Mayer-Vietoris square (with the middle pair `S.X₂` and `S.X₃`
290	biproduct-summed). The two morphisms are: `f` (the difference `(yoneda.map S.f₁₂)
291	– (yoneda.map S.f₁₃)` lifted through the biproduct), and `g` (the sum
292	`(yoneda.map S.f₂₄) + (yoneda.map S.f₃₄)` desced through the biproduct).
293	The `zero` proof (i.e.\ `f ≫ g = 0`) follows from the
294	`cokernelCofork.condition` of the pushout square in
295	`HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf` above.
296	
297	Probe-confirmed body (iter-019 plan-agent); structure-literal mirror of Mathlib L235–249
298	with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`. The `@[simps]` attribute generates
299	field-projection simp lemmas (`HModule'_shortComplex_X₁`, `..._X₂`, `..._X₃`,
300	`..._f`, `..._g`) consumed by the iter-020+ `Mono`/`Epi`/`Exact`/`ShortExact`
301	proofs and the iter-022+ `δ` definition. -/
302	@[simps]
303	noncomputable def HModule'_shortComplex
304	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
305	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
306	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
307	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
308	    ShortComplex (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) where
309	  X₁ := (presheafToSheaf J _).obj (yoneda.obj S.X₁ ⋙ ModuleCat.free k)
310	  X₂ := (presheafToSheaf J _).obj (yoneda.obj S.X₂ ⋙ ModuleCat.free k) ⊞
311	    (presheafToSheaf J _).obj (yoneda.obj S.X₃ ⋙ ModuleCat.free k)
312	  X₃ := (presheafToSheaf J _).obj (yoneda.obj S.X₄ ⋙ ModuleCat.free k)
313	  f :=
314	    biprod.lift
315	      ((presheafToSheaf J _).map (Functor.whiskerRight (yoneda.map S.f₁₂) _))
316	      (-(presheafToSheaf J _).map (Functor.whiskerRight (yoneda.map S.f₁₃) _))
317	  g :=
318	    biprod.desc
319	      ((presheafToSheaf J _).map (Functor.whiskerRight (yoneda.map S.f₂₄) _))
320	      ((presheafToSheaf J _).map (Functor.whiskerRight (yoneda.map S.f₃₄) _))
321	  zero :=
322	    (S.map (yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k) ⋙
323	        presheafToSheaf J _)).cokernelCofork.condition
324	
325	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-020 helper, Mathlib gap-fill): the free-module
326	functor `ModuleCat.free k : Type u ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k` preserves monomorphisms.
327	Mathlib registers `AddCommGrpCat.instPreservesMonomorphismsFree :
328	AddCommGrpCat.free.PreservesMonomorphisms` in
329	`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/Grp/Adjunctions.lean` but does not register the
330	corresponding instance for `ModuleCat.free k` in
331	`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Adjunctions.lean`. This project-local
332	instance fills the gap so that, after `simp only [biprod.lift_snd]`, the
333	typeclass-search engine can discharge the residual `Mono` goal in the proof
334	of `HModule'_shortComplex_f_mono` below.
335	
336	The proof: for an injective function `f : X → Y` between types,
337	`(ModuleCat.free k).map f = ModuleCat.ofHom (Finsupp.lmapDomain k k f)` (defeq),
338	and `Finsupp.mapDomain f` is injective on `Finsupp X k → Finsupp Y k` by
339	Mathlib's `Finsupp.mapDomain_injective`. Bridging via `ModuleCat.mono_iff_injective`
340	recovers `Mono` in `ModuleCat k`. -/
341	instance ModuleCat_free_preservesMonomorphisms
342	    (k : Type u) [Field k] : (ModuleCat.free k).PreservesMonomorphisms := by
343	  refine ⟨fun {X Y} f hf ↦ ?_⟩
344	  have hf' : Function.Injective f := (CategoryTheory.mono_iff_injective f).mp hf
345	  rw [ModuleCat.mono_iff_injective]
346	  exact Finsupp.mapDomain_injective hf'
347	
348	-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-020): `(HModule'_shortComplex k S).f` is a
349	-- monomorphism in `Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`. Direct mirror of Mathlib's
350	-- `MayerVietorisSquare.lean` L251–257 with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`.
351	-- The `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` attribute is
352	-- required because the typeclass-search engine needs to unfold the `dsimp`-normal
353	-- form of `(HModule'_shortComplex k S).f` past structure-literal projection.
354	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
355	instance HModule'_shortComplex_f_mono
356	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
357	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
358	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
359	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
360	    Mono (HModule'_shortComplex k S).f := by
361	  have : Mono ((HModule'_shortComplex k S).f ≫ biprod.snd) := by
362	    dsimp
363	    simp only [biprod.lift_snd]
364	    infer_instance
365	  exact mono_of_mono _ biprod.snd
366	
367	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-020): `(HModule'_shortComplex k S).g` is an
368	epimorphism in `Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`. Direct mirror of Mathlib's
369	`MayerVietorisSquare.lean` L259–261 with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`.
370	The proof is a one-line term-mode body using
371	`ShortComplex.exact_and_epi_g_iff_g_is_cokernel` and the iter-019 lemma
372	`HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf`'s `isColimitCokernelCofork` accessor. -/
373	instance HModule'_shortComplex_g_epi
374	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
375	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
376	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
377	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
378	    Epi (HModule'_shortComplex k S).g :=
379	  ((HModule'_shortComplex k S).exact_and_epi_g_iff_g_is_cokernel.2
380	    ⟨(HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf k S).isColimitCokernelCofork⟩).2
381	
382	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-020): `(HModule'_shortComplex k S).Exact`,
383	i.e. the kernel of `g` equals the image of `f` in `Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`.
384	Direct mirror of Mathlib's `MayerVietorisSquare.lean` L263–265 with
385	`AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`. The proof is a one-line term-mode
386	body using `ShortComplex.exact_of_g_is_cokernel` and the iter-019 lemma
387	`HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf`'s `isColimitCokernelCofork` accessor. -/
388	lemma HModule'_shortComplex_exact
389	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
390	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
391	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
392	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
393	    (HModule'_shortComplex k S).Exact :=
394	  ShortComplex.exact_of_g_is_cokernel _
395	    (HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf k S).isColimitCokernelCofork
396	
397	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-020): `(HModule'_shortComplex k S).ShortExact`,
398	the short-exact predicate (combining `Mono f`, `Epi g`, and `Exact`) in
399	`Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`. Direct mirror of Mathlib's `MayerVietorisSquare.lean`
400	L267–268 with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`. The proof is a one-line
401	anonymous-constructor: the `Mono f` and `Epi g` predicates are typeclass-resolved
402	from `HModule'_shortComplex_f_mono` and `HModule'_shortComplex_g_epi`, leaving
403	only the `exact` field which is filled with `HModule'_shortComplex_exact`. The
404	named lemma is consumed in iter-021+ as `S.HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact.extClass`
405	to define the connecting hom `HModule'_δ` of the Mayer-Vietoris LES. -/
406	lemma HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact
407	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
408	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
409	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
410	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
411	    (HModule'_shortComplex k S).ShortExact where
412	  exact := HModule'_shortComplex_exact k S
413	
414	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-021): the connecting homomorphism `δ` of the
415	Mayer-Vietoris long exact sequence in `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf cohomology.
416	For a Mayer-Vietoris square `S` and adjacent cohomological degrees `n₀ + 1 = n₁`,
417	the morphism
418	  δ : (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n₀).obj (op S.X₁) ⟶
419	        (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n₁).obj (op S.X₄)
420	in `AddCommGrpCat` is the precomposition with the extension class
421	`(HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact k S).extClass : Ext (...X₁...) (...X₃...) 1`
422	followed by `AddCommGrpCat.ofHom` to wrap the resulting `AddMonoidHom` as a
423	categorical morphism. Direct mirror of Mathlib's `MayerVietorisSquare.δ`
424	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` L112–114)
425	for the `ModuleCat k` flavor with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`.
426	
427	The `[HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]` typeclass is required (the
428	`Ext.precomp` operation depends on it) and matches the corresponding
429	requirement on iter-016 `HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor` / `..._cohomologyPresheaf`,
430	iter-017 `HModule'_toBiprod` / `..._fromBiprod`, and iter-018
431	`HModule'_toBiprod_fromBiprod`.
432	
433	This connecting hom is the missing third link of the Mayer-Vietoris exact
434	sequence: combined with iter-017's `toBiprod` (sum of restriction) and
435	`fromBiprod` (difference of restriction), iter-018's `toBiprod ≫ fromBiprod = 0`,
436	and the iter-022+ packaging into a `ComposableArrows`-form sequence + iter-023+
437	sequence-iso to `Ext.contravariantSequence` + iter-024+ exactness theorem, it
438	will yield the full LES `... → Hⁿ(X₄) → Hⁿ(X₂) ⊞ Hⁿ(X₃) → Hⁿ(X₁) → Hⁿ⁺¹(X₄) → ...`
439	in `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf cohomology. -/
440	noncomputable def HModule'_δ
441	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
442	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
443	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
444	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
445	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
446	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
447	    (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n₀).obj (Opposite.op S.X₁) ⟶
448	      (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n₁).obj (Opposite.op S.X₄) :=
449	  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact k S).extClass.precomp _ (by omega))
450	
451	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-022): the Mayer-Vietoris long exact sequence
452	in `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf cohomology, packaged as a `ComposableArrows` of
453	length 5 in `AddCommGrpCat`. The five composable morphisms are, in order,
454	`HModule'_toBiprod` at degree `n₀`, `HModule'_fromBiprod` at degree `n₀`, the
455	connecting hom `HModule'_δ` from degree `n₀` to degree `n₁`, `HModule'_toBiprod`
456	at degree `n₁`, and `HModule'_fromBiprod` at degree `n₁`. Direct mirror of
457	Mathlib's `MayerVietorisSquare.sequence`
458	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` L120–122)
459	for the `ModuleCat k` flavor.
460	
461	The `noncomputable abbrev` form is load-bearing: downstream `dsimp`-based
462	unfolding (in iter-023+ `HModule'_sequenceIso` and iter-024+
463	`HModule'_sequence_exact`) needs to access `mk₅`'s field-projection simp
464	lemmas through the abbrev. -/
465	noncomputable abbrev HModule'_sequence
466	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
467	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
468	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
469	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
470	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
471	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
472	    ComposableArrows AddCommGrpCat 5 :=
473	  ComposableArrows.mk₅ (HModule'_toBiprod k S F n₀) (HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n₀)
474	    (HModule'_δ k S F n₀ n₁ h)
475	    (HModule'_toBiprod k S F n₁) (HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n₁)
476	
477	/-- Iter-023 aux lemma 1: explicit elementwise formula for `HModule'_toBiprod`
478	(mirror Mathlib `MayerVietoris.lean` L48–64). -/
479	lemma HModule'_toBiprod_apply
480	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
481	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
482	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
483	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
484	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
485	    {n : ℕ} (y : (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₄)) :
486	    HModule'_toBiprod k S F n y =
487	      (AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).inv
488	        ⟨(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₂₄.op y,
489	          (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₃₄.op y⟩ := by
490	  apply (AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv.injective
491	  dsimp [HModule'_toBiprod]
492	  ext
493	  · rw [Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply, Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply,
494	      ← AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd_inv_comp_fst_apply, Iso.hom_inv_id_apply,
495	      ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, biprod.lift_fst, Iso.inv_hom_id_apply]
496	  · rw [Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply, Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply,
497	      ← AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd_inv_comp_snd_apply, Iso.hom_inv_id_apply,
498	      ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, biprod.lift_snd, Iso.inv_hom_id_apply]
499	
500	/-- Iter-023 aux lemma 2: explicit elementwise formula for `HModule'_fromBiprod`
501	on the inverse of `biprodIsoProd` (mirror Mathlib L77–83). -/
502	lemma HModule'_fromBiprod_biprodIsoProd_inv_apply
503	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
504	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
505	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
506	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
507	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
508	    {n : ℕ}
509	    (y₁ : (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂))
510	    (y₂ : (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₃)) :
511	    HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n
512	        ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).inv ⟨y₁, y₂⟩) =
513	      (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₂.op y₁ -
514	        (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₃.op y₂ := by
515	  dsimp [HModule'_fromBiprod]
516	  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]
517	  simp [AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd_inv_comp_desc, sub_eq_add_neg]
518	
519	-- Iter-023 aux lemma 3: bridges `AddCommGrpCat`-side `biprodIsoProd` and
520	-- `Ext`-side `Ext.biprodAddEquiv` for the `toBiprod` morphism
521	-- (mirror Mathlib L85–91). The `set_option ... in` and `attribute ... in`
522	-- wrappers match Mathlib L85, L86 verbatim.
523	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
524	attribute [local simp] HModule'_toBiprod_apply in
525	lemma HModule'_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom_toBiprod_apply
526	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
527	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
528	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
529	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
530	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
531	    {n : ℕ} (x : (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₄)) :
532	    Abelian.Ext.biprodAddEquiv.symm
533	        ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).hom (HModule'_toBiprod k S F n x)) =
534	      (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ (HModule'_shortComplex k S).g).comp x (zero_add n) :=
535	  Abelian.Ext.biprodAddEquiv.injective (by cat_disch)
536	
537	-- Iter-023 aux lemma 4: bridges the same machinery for the `fromBiprod`
538	-- morphism (mirror Mathlib L93–106).
539	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
540	attribute [local simp] sub_eq_add_neg in
541	lemma HModule'_mk₀_f_comp_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom
542	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
543	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
544	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
545	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
546	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
547	    {n : ℕ}
548	    (x : ↑((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂) ⊞
549	           (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₃))) :
550	    (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ (HModule'_shortComplex k S).f).comp
551	        (Abelian.Ext.biprodAddEquiv.symm
552	          ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).hom x)) (zero_add n) =
553	      (HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n x) := by
554	  obtain ⟨⟨x₂, x₃⟩, rfl⟩ :=
555	    (AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv.symm.surjective x
556	  dsimp
557	  rw [Abelian.Ext.biprodAddEquiv_symm_apply,
558	    Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_symm_apply,
559	    HModule'_fromBiprod_biprodIsoProd_inv_apply]
560	  cat_disch
561	
562	-- Iter-023 main: comparison iso from the iter-022 LES sequence to
563	-- `Ext.contravariantSequence` (mirror Mathlib L124–138). The technical heart
564	-- of the Mayer-Vietoris LES.
565	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
566	noncomputable def HModule'_sequenceIso
567	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
568	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
569	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
570	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
571	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
572	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
573	    HModule'_sequence k S F n₀ n₁ h ≅
574	      Abelian.Ext.contravariantSequence (HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact k S)
575	        F n₀ n₁ (by omega) :=
576	  ComposableArrows.isoMk₅ (Iso.refl _)
577	    ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).trans
578	      (Abelian.Ext.biprodAddEquiv.symm).toAddCommGrpIso)
579	    (Iso.refl _) (Iso.refl _)
580	    ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).trans
581	      (Abelian.Ext.biprodAddEquiv.symm).toAddCommGrpIso)
582	    (Iso.refl _)
583	    (by ext; apply HModule'_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom_toBiprod_apply)
584	    (by ext; symm; apply HModule'_mk₀_f_comp_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom)
585	    (by dsimp; rw [Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp]; rfl)
586	    (by ext; apply HModule'_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom_toBiprod_apply)
587	    (by ext; symm; apply HModule'_mk₀_f_comp_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom)
588	
589	/-- Iter-026: Mayer-Vietoris LES exactness theorem (mirror Mathlib
590	`MayerVietoris.lean` L140–141). The iter-022 sequence is exact via the iter-023
591	comparison iso to `Ext.contravariantSequence`. -/
592	lemma HModule'_sequence_exact
593	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
594	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
595	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
596	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
597	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
598	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
599	    (HModule'_sequence k S F n₀ n₁ h).Exact :=
600	  ComposableArrows.exact_of_iso (HModule'_sequenceIso k S F n₀ n₁ h).symm
601	    (Abelian.Ext.contravariantSequence_exact _ _ _ _ _)
602	
603	/-- Iter-026: `δ ≫ toBiprod = 0` simp companion (mirror Mathlib L143–145). -/
604	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
605	lemma HModule'_δ_toBiprod
606	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
607	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
608	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
609	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
610	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
611	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
612	    HModule'_δ k S F n₀ n₁ h ≫ HModule'_toBiprod k S F n₁ = 0 :=
613	  (HModule'_sequence_exact k S F n₀ n₁ h).zero 2
614	
615	/-- Iter-026: `fromBiprod ≫ δ = 0` simp companion (mirror Mathlib L147–149). -/
616	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
617	lemma HModule'_fromBiprod_δ
618	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
619	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
620	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
621	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
622	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
623	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
624	    HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n₀ ≫ HModule'_δ k S F n₀ n₁ h = 0 :=
625	  (HModule'_sequence_exact k S F n₀ n₁ h).zero 1
626	
627	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.MayerVietorisCore
7	
8	/-!
9	# Mayer-Vietoris on a 2-affine cover and Čech-acyclic cover infrastructure
10	
11	This file (iter-063 split, cohort 2) contains the 2-affine cover
12	Mayer-Vietoris theorems, the cover-totality bridges (universe-shift
13	`LinearEquiv`s, `finrank` / `Module.Finite` transports), the
14	`IsCechAcyclicCover` consumers, the comparison-iso typeclass
15	`HasCechToHModuleIso`, and the existence-bundled
16	`HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` predicate together with the producer that
17	fires `IsAffineHModuleVanishing` from a `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover`
18	hypothesis.
19	
20	This is cohort 2 of the original `MayerVietoris.lean` split:
21	* `MayerVietorisCore.lean`: MV LES core + Mathlib gap-fill.
22	* `MayerVietorisCover.lean` (this file): 2-affine cover MV +
23	  cover-totality bridges + `IsCechAcyclicCover` consumers +
24	  `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` carrier.
25	
26	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex`.
27	-/
28	
29	set_option autoImplicit false
30	
31	universe u v w w'
32	
33	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
34	
35	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
36	
37	section AffineCoverMVSquare
38	
39	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-028): a bundled 2-affine cover of a scheme
40	together with the affineness of the pairwise intersection. The geometric input
41	to the abstract Mayer-Vietoris LES `HModule'_sequence_exact` consumes — packaged
42	to record the affineness conditions and the cover-totality hypothesis that the
43	abstract MV-square does not capture. The accessor `toMayerVietorisSquare`
44	produces the underlying Mathlib `MayerVietorisSquare`.
45	
46	Mathematically: a 2-affine open cover of `X` whose pairwise intersection is also
47	affine. The cover hypothesis `cover : U₁ ⊔ U₂ = ⊤` is load-bearing for downstream
48	Serre-finiteness use (it identifies the `MayerVietorisSquare` corner `X₄` with
49	the whole scheme `X`). -/
50	structure AffineCoverMVSquare (X : Scheme.{u}) where
51	  /-- First affine open of the cover. -/
52	  U₁ : X.Opens
53	  /-- Second affine open of the cover. -/
54	  U₂ : X.Opens
55	  /-- `U₁` is affine. -/
56	  isAffineOpen_U₁ : IsAffineOpen U₁
57	  /-- `U₂` is affine. -/
58	  isAffineOpen_U₂ : IsAffineOpen U₂
59	  /-- The intersection `U₁ ⊓ U₂` is affine. -/
60	  isAffineOpen_inf : IsAffineOpen (U₁ ⊓ U₂)
61	  /-- Total cover: `U₁ ⊔ U₂ = ⊤`. -/
62	  cover : U₁ ⊔ U₂ = ⊤
63	
64	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-028): the underlying abstract Mayer-Vietoris
65	square produced by a 2-affine cover. Bridge from the geometric input (an
66	`AffineCoverMVSquare`) to the categorical input (a `MayerVietorisSquare` for
67	the Grothendieck topology on `Opens X.toTopCat`) the abstract MV-LES theorem
68	`HModule'_sequence_exact` consumes. The four corners collapse definitionally:
69	`X₁ = U₁ ⊓ U₂`, `X₂ = U₁`, `X₃ = U₂`, `X₄ = U₁ ⊔ U₂` (each by `rfl`,
70	plan-agent probe-verified). -/
71	noncomputable def AffineCoverMVSquare.toMayerVietorisSquare {X : Scheme.{u}}
72	    (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
73	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat).MayerVietorisSquare :=
74	  Opens.mayerVietorisSquare S.U₁ S.U₂
75	
76	/-- Iter-029 corner identification: `X₁ = U₁ ⊓ U₂`. The first three corners are
77	`rfl`-equal because `Opens.mayerVietorisSquare` defines them definitionally. -/
78	@[simp]
79	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X₁
80	    {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
81	    S.toMayerVietorisSquare.toSquare.X₁ = S.U₁ ⊓ S.U₂ := rfl
82	
83	/-- Iter-029 corner identification: `X₂ = U₁`. -/
84	@[simp]
85	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X₂
86	    {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
87	    S.toMayerVietorisSquare.toSquare.X₂ = S.U₁ := rfl

91	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X₃

106	for reusability; the `toModuleKSheaf C` application is iter-030+ work. The

131	The dot-notation `S.HModule'_sequence` resolves cleanly via method-call

145	`toModuleKSheaf C`. -/

154	
155	/-- Iter-031 cover-totality source-object identification. For a category `C`
156	with terminal object `T`, the sheafification of the free `k`-module on the
157	representable presheaf at `T` is canonically isomorphic to the constant sheaf
158	at `ModuleCat.of k k`. This is the natural-iso prerequisite for identifying
159	`HModule' k F n T` (open-evaluation cohomology at the terminal `T`, iter-014)
160	with `HModule k F n` (global cohomology, iter-009): both are `Ext` groups
161	whose source objects are made canonically isomorphic by this iso.
162	
163	Assembled from three Mathlib pieces, mirroring the structure
164	`constantSheaf J D = Functor.const Cᵒᵖ ⋙ presheafToSheaf J D`:
165	1. `yoneda.obj T ≅ (Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).obj PUnit` — the terminal-collapse
166	   of the representable, by `IsTerminal.from`/`IsTerminal.hom_ext`.
167	2. `Functor.constComp _ PUnit (ModuleCat.free k)` — the identity
168	   `(Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).obj PUnit ⋙ ModuleCat.free k =
169	    (Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).obj ((ModuleCat.free k).obj PUnit)`
170	   packaged as an iso.
171	3. `(Finsupp.LinearEquiv.finsuppUnique k k PUnit).toModuleIso` —
172	   `(ModuleCat.free k).obj PUnit ≅ ModuleCat.of k k`. -/
173	noncomputable def HModule'_top_sourceIso
174	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
175	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
176	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
177	    {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T) :
178	    (presheafToSheaf J _).obj
179	        ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj T)
180	      ≅ (constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) :=
181	  (presheafToSheaf J _).mapIso
182	    (Functor.isoWhiskerRight
183	        (NatIso.ofComponents
184	          (fun _ => Equiv.toIso { toFun := fun _ => PUnit.unit
185	                                  invFun := fun _ => hT.from _
186	                                  left_inv := fun _ => hT.hom_ext _ _
187	                                  right_inv := fun _ => rfl })
188	          (fun _ => by ext; rfl))
189	        (ModuleCat.free k) ≪≫
190	      Functor.constComp _ PUnit.{u+1} (ModuleCat.free k) ≪≫
191	      (Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).mapIso
192	        (Finsupp.LinearEquiv.finsuppUnique k k PUnit).toModuleIso)
193	
194	/-- Iter-032 cover-totality Ext-transport at universe `u+1`. Combining the
195	iter-031 `HModule'_top_sourceIso` (a sheaf-level natural iso between source
196	objects of two `Ext` computations) with the Mathlib Ext-API operation
197	`precompOfLinear` (pre-composition of `Ext` along an `Ext 0`-element) yields
198	a `k`-linear equivalence between the universe-`u+1` Ext at the sheafified
199	representable on `T` and `HModule k F n`.
200	
201	The universe annotation `Abelian.Ext.{u+1}` on the LHS is load-bearing —
202	without it Lean would pick `Ext.{u}` (matching iter-014's `HModule'` choice),
203	and the equivalence would not typecheck against `HModule k F n : Type (u+1)`
204	(iter-009). By pinning the LHS to `Ext.{u+1}` we sidestep the iter-031
205	universe mismatch (`HModule' : Type u` vs `HModule : Type (u+1)`); both
206	sides of this equivalence live at `Type (u+1)`. The bridge from
207	`HModule' k F n T : Type u` to the LHS here is iter-033+ work — most
208	plausibly via a small refactor on iter-014 to align `HModule'`'s universe,
209	or via a `ULift.{u+1}` wrapper.
210	
211	Body uses `LinearEquiv.ofLinear` with two pre-composition maps via the
212	iter-031 iso's hom/inv. The round-trip equations close by a four-step
213	rewrite: associativity (with the middle factor degree 0), composition
214	collapse via `mk₀_comp_mk₀`, the iso identity, and `mk₀_id_comp`. -/
215	noncomputable def HModule_top_linearEquiv
216	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
217	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
218	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
219	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
220	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T) :
221	    Abelian.Ext.{u+1}
222	        ((presheafToSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
223	          ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj T)) F n
224	      ≃ₗ[k] HModule k F n :=
225	  let α := HModule'_top_sourceIso k hT
226	  LinearEquiv.ofLinear
227	    ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n))
228	    ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n))
229	    (by
230	      ext x
231	      change (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).comp
232	        ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).comp x (zero_add n)) (zero_add n) = x
233	      rw [← Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀,
234	          α.inv_hom_id, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp])
235	    (by
236	      ext y
237	      change (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).comp
238	        ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).comp y (zero_add n)) (zero_add n) = y
239	      rw [← Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀,
240	          α.hom_inv_id, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp])
241	
242	/-- Iter-033 cover-totality Ext-transport at universe `u`. The universe-`u`
243	parallel of iter-032's `HModule_top_linearEquiv`. Combining the iter-031
244	`HModule'_top_sourceIso` (a sheaf-level natural iso between source objects
245	of two `Ext` computations) with the Mathlib Ext-API operation
246	`precompOfLinear` (pre-composition of `Ext` along an `Ext 0`-element)
247	yields a `k`-linear equivalence between `HModule' k F n T` (the iter-014
248	universe-`u` cohomology of an open at the terminal) and the universe-`u`
249	`Ext` at the constant sheaf on `ModuleCat.of k k`.
250	
251	Both sides live at `Type u` (matching iter-014's `HModule'` ascription).
252	This is the universe-`u` parallel of iter-032's universe-`u+1` form; the
253	final bridge between the two (`Ext.{u}((constantSheaf J _).obj
254	(ModuleCat.of k k)) F n ≃ₗ[k] HModule k F n = Ext.{u+1}(...)`) is
255	iter-034+ work, requiring Mathlib's `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv` (a bare
256	`Equiv`, file `Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Basic.lean`
257	L540) to be upgraded to a `LinearEquiv`.
258	
259	The body is shape-identical to iter-032: `LinearEquiv.ofLinear` with two
260	pre-composition maps via the iter-031 iso's hom/inv. The round-trip
261	equations close by the same four-step rewrite chain. Lean's universe
262	inference adapts automatically because the LHS `HModule' k F n T`
263	forces `Ext.{u}` everywhere. -/
264	noncomputable def HModule'_top_linearEquiv
265	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
266	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
267	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
268	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
269	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T) :
270	    HModule' k F n T
271	      ≃ₗ[k] Abelian.Ext ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
272	              (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n :=
273	  let α := HModule'_top_sourceIso k hT
274	  LinearEquiv.ofLinear
275	    ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n))
276	    ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n))
277	    (by
278	      ext x
279	      change (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).comp
280	        ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).comp x (zero_add n)) (zero_add n) = x
281	      rw [← Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀,
282	          α.inv_hom_id, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp])
283	    (by
284	      ext y
285	      change (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).comp
286	        ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).comp y (zero_add n)) (zero_add n) = y
287	      rw [← Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀,
288	          α.hom_inv_id, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp])
289	
290	/-- Iter-034 universe-bump bridge. Composes iter-033's
291	`HModule'_top_linearEquiv` (universe-`u` cover-totality between `HModule' k F n T`
292	and `Ext.{u} ((constantSheaf J _).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n`) with the
293	universe shift `Abelian.Ext.chgUnivLinearEquiv` (Mathlib gap-fill at the top
294	of this file) lifting the result to `Ext.{u+1} ((constantSheaf J _).obj
295	(ModuleCat.of k k)) F n = HModule k F n` (definitional unfold of the iter-009
296	abbrev). The composition gives the full bridge `HModule' k F n T ≃ₗ[k]
297	HModule k F n` for terminal `T`, closing Step 3 of the Serre-finiteness
298	scaffold. Iter-035+ specialises this to `AffineCoverMVSquare` using
299	iter-029's `toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X₄ : ... = ⊤` simp lemma. -/
300	noncomputable def HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv
301	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
302	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
303	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
304	    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
305	    [HasExt.{u + 1} (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
306	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T) :
307	    HModule' k F n T ≃ₗ[k] HModule k F n :=
308	  (HModule'_top_linearEquiv k F n hT).trans Abelian.Ext.chgUnivLinearEquiv
309	
310	/-- Iter-035 corner-bridge specialisation, abstract sheaf form. With iter-034's
311	universal bridge `HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv` in scope, the natural
312	specialisation lands the bridge on the `X₄` corner of an `AffineCoverMVSquare`,
313	where iter-029's simp lemma `toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X₄` identifies the
314	corner with `⊤ : Opens X.toTopCat`. Mathlib's `Preorder.isTerminalTop`
315	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/Preorder.lean`) supplies the
316	`IsTerminal ⊤` witness; transporting this back via the simp lemma's `.symm`
317	gives the required `IsTerminal X₄` to feed into the universal bridge. The
318	qualified `TopologicalSpace.Opens X.toTopCat` form is required to sidestep the
319	iter-029 sub-namespace shadowing trap (bare `Opens X.toTopCat` inside `def
320	AffineCoverMVSquare.foo` shadows to `Scheme.Opens` which expects a `Scheme`). -/
321	noncomputable def AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_X₄_linearEquiv
322	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare)
323	    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
324	    [HasExt.{u + 1} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
325	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
326	    HModule' k F n S.toMayerVietorisSquare.toSquare.X₄ ≃ₗ[k] HModule k F n :=
327	  HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv k F n
328	    (S.toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X₄.symm ▸
329	      (Preorder.isTerminalTop (TopologicalSpace.Opens X.toTopCat)))
330	
331	/-- Iter-035 corner-bridge curve specialisation. Direct application of
332	`AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_X₄_linearEquiv` to `F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`
333	for `C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))`, via the dot-notation method-call form
334	`S.HModule'_X₄_linearEquiv` (mirrors the iter-030 `HModule'_sequence_curve`
335	pattern: dot-notation method-call resolves cleanly via lookup against
336	`S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare`, sidestepping the iter-029 sub-namespace
337	shadowing trap that required `_root_.` disambiguation in the abstract
338	`_sequence` body). -/
339	noncomputable def AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_X₄_linearEquiv_curve
340	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))}
341	    (S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare)
342	    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
343	    [HasExt.{u + 1} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
344	    (n : ℕ) :
345	    HModule' k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n S.toMayerVietorisSquare.toSquare.X₄
346	      ≃ₗ[k] HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n :=
347	  S.HModule'_X₄_linearEquiv k _ n
348	
349	/-- Iter-036 finrank corollary, abstract sheaf form. With iter-035's corner-bridge
350	`HModule'_X₄_linearEquiv` in scope, the immediate algebraic consequence is that the
351	genus carrier `Module.finrank k (HModule k F n)` equals the open-evaluated form
352	`Module.finrank k (HModule' k F n X₄)`, by Mathlib's `LinearEquiv.finrank_eq`
353	applied to the bridge. The `.symm` is required: the bridge is
354	`HModule' k F n X₄ ≃ₗ[k] HModule k F n` (LHS-to-RHS), so the symmetric form
355	gives the orientation wanted by `genus C := Module.finrank k (HModule k _ 1)`. -/
356	theorem AffineCoverMVSquare.finrank_HModule_eq_HModule'_X₄
357	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare)
358	    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
359	    [HasExt.{u + 1} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
360	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
361	    Module.finrank k (HModule k F n) =
362	      Module.finrank k (HModule' k F n S.toMayerVietorisSquare.toSquare.X₄) :=
363	  (S.HModule'_X₄_linearEquiv k F n).symm.finrank_eq
364	
365	/-- Iter-036 finrank corollary, curve specialisation. Direct application of (a) to
366	`F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` for `C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))`, via the
367	dot-notation method-call form `S.finrank_HModule_eq_HModule'_X₄` (mirrors the
368	iter-030 `_sequence_curve` and iter-035 `_X₄_linearEquiv_curve` patterns: dot-notation
369	method-call resolves cleanly via lookup against `S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare`,
370	sidestepping the iter-029 sub-namespace shadowing trap). -/
371	theorem AffineCoverMVSquare.finrank_HModule_eq_HModule'_X₄_curve
372	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))}
373	    (S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare)
374	    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
375	    [HasExt.{u + 1} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
376	    (n : ℕ) :
377	    Module.finrank k (HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n) =
378	      Module.finrank k (HModule' k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n
379	        S.toMayerVietorisSquare.toSquare.X₄) :=
380	  S.finrank_HModule_eq_HModule'_X₄ k _ n
381	
382	/-- Iter-037 `Module.Finite` transport companion, abstract sheaf form. With iter-035's
383	corner-bridge `HModule'_X₄_linearEquiv : HModule' k F n X₄ ≃ₗ[k] HModule k F n`
384	in scope (LHS = X₄ form, RHS = global form), Mathlib's `Module.Finite.equiv`
385	transports finiteness from LHS to RHS: given `Module.Finite k (HModule' k F n X₄)`
386	as a hypothesis, we get `Module.Finite k (HModule k F n)` as a conclusion.
387	**No `.symm` is needed** — `Module.Finite.equiv` consumes the hypothesis on the
388	LHS of the equiv (`[Module.Finite R M]`) and produces the conclusion on the RHS
389	(`Module.Finite R N`), which is exactly the orientation `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`
390	(Jacobian.lean) eventually consumes. -/
391	theorem AffineCoverMVSquare.module_finite_HModule_of_HModule'_X₄
392	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare)
393	    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
394	    [HasExt.{u + 1} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
395	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ)
396	    [Module.Finite k (HModule' k F n S.toMayerVietorisSquare.toSquare.X₄)] :
397	    Module.Finite k (HModule k F n) :=
398	  Module.Finite.equiv (S.HModule'_X₄_linearEquiv k F n)
399	
400	/-- Iter-037 `Module.Finite` transport companion, curve specialisation. Direct
401	application of (a) to `F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` for `C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))`,
402	via the dot-notation method-call form `S.module_finite_HModule_of_HModule'_X₄`
403	(mirrors the iter-030 `_sequence_curve`, iter-035 `_X₄_linearEquiv_curve`, and
404	iter-036 `_finrank_HModule_eq_HModule'_X₄_curve` patterns: dot-notation method-call
405	resolves cleanly via lookup against `S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare`, sidestepping
406	the iter-029 sub-namespace shadowing trap). -/
407	theorem AffineCoverMVSquare.module_finite_HModule_of_HModule'_X₄_curve
408	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))}
409	    (S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare)
410	    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
411	    [HasExt.{u + 1} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
412	    (n : ℕ)
413	    [Module.Finite k (HModule' k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n
